_meta.purpose: Each packet is a little machine with inputs (things it produces) and payoffs (things it spends). Pairs score high when one half creates what the other half consumes. This dictionary defines those machines and how to score them.

tags.label: Blink and enters value
payoffCards: creatures and permanents that create value when they enter or leave
supportCards: cheap creatures with strong enters abilities
tags.failureRisk: drawing blink support without enough good enters targets
tags.scoringNotes: Score very high when both packets contain creatures with enters abilities and at least one contains repeatable blink/flicker support.

tags.label: Equipment combat
supportCards: cheap resilient creatures to carry gear
tags.failureRisk: removal on the equipped creature costing heavy tempo
conflictsWith: sacrifice packets with few lasting bodies
tags.scoringNotes: Score very high when both packets include Equipment and enough creatures to hold it.

tags.label: Artifact board
tags.failureRisk: artifact removal or a slow start with no early board
conflictsWith: packets with few artifacts and no artifact payoffs
tags.scoringNotes: Score high when the combined deck has artifact density and multiple ways to turn artifacts into pressure.

tags.label: Noncreature spell chain
tags.failureRisk: a weak early board and drawing the wrong half at the wrong time
conflictsWith: creature-heavy packets with few instants and sorceries
tags.scoringNotes: Give a ceiling bonus for spell density and a consistency penalty when early board presence is low.

tags.label: Wide combat
tags.failureRisk: stalling into sweepers or oversized blockers
tags.scoringNotes: Score high when both packets produce multiple bodies and reward attacking with several creatures.

tags.label: Graveyard value
tags.failureRisk: graveyard hate and clunky early turns
tags.scoringNotes: Score high when one packet fills the graveyard and the other converts it into cards, creatures, or damage.

tags.label: Draw and card-flow payoffs
payoffCards: cards that reward drawing extra cards
tags.failureRisk: durdling and drawing cards without affecting the board
conflictsWith: very low-curve aggro packets with no card flow
tags.scoringNotes: Score high when the combined deck can draw extra cards without losing the board.

tags.label: Life-gain creature stability
tags.failureRisk: a lower ceiling than combo, artifact, or spell pairs
conflictsWith: all-in spell packets that barely attack or block
tags.scoringNotes: Score high for durability, creature count, and repeated life gain.
